arguments.task: Open official Otter AI pages to verify API availability, authentication methods, and pricing/access. Prefer first-party pages such as help.otter.ai, otter.ai, and any official developer or API documentation pages. Read page content, do not rely on search snippets. Find canonical URLs that directly support: what Otter AI is, whether Otter provides a public API and its auth methods, any pricing or plan page relevant to API/self-serve access, and whether there is any official MCP/server mention. Return a concise evidence-backed summary with the exact URLs visited and what each page proves. If documentation is JS-rendered, missing, contradictory, or too thin to decide a structured field, say so explicitly.

input.thought: Use the browser to inspect official Otter AI pages and extract evidence-backed documentation details.

raw_result.description: AI meeting assistant for transcription, summaries, and searchable meeting knowledge.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official Otter help documentation for the Public API indicates Bearer Token authentication. No first-party evidence was verified for OAuth2, API keys, or Basic auth.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official pricing states 'Otter API & Webhooks' are available on the Enterprise plan, so API access is not self-serve on free/basic tiers.

api_surface.breadth: Public API availability is documented, but the verified first-party pages reviewed here only establish existence, bearer-token auth, and Enterprise-plan gating rather than a broad endpoint catalog.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official pricing lists an 'Otter MCP server' feature, indicating an official MCP offering exists.

raw_result.blocker: API access appears restricted to the Enterprise plan, requiring paid upgrade/sales motion before building against the official API.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit details were verified from the official pages reviewed.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official pricing publicly exposes both 'Otter API & Webhooks' and an 'Otter MCP server' feature, which is a strong signal of agent/tooling relevance even though API access is plan-gated.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The browser observed an Otter help article describing Public API bearer-token auth, but the direct canonical article URL encountered 404 behavior during targeted recheck, so the structured auth_methods field could not be fully re-verified from a stable canonical page fetch in this pass.

result.description: AI meeting assistant for transcription, summaries, and searchable meeting knowledge.
result.auth_notes: Official Otter help documentation for the Public API indicates Bearer Token authentication. No first-party evidence was verified for OAuth2, API keys, or Basic auth.
result.gating_reason: Official pricing states 'Otter API & Webhooks' are available on the Enterprise plan, so API access is not self-serve on free/basic tiers.

api_surface.breadth: Public API availability is documented, but the verified first-party pages reviewed here only establish existence, bearer-token auth, and Enterprise-plan gating rather than a broad endpoint catalog.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official pricing lists an 'Otter MCP server' feature, indicating an official MCP offering exists.

result.blocker: API access appears restricted to the Enterprise plan, requiring paid upgrade/sales motion before building against the official API.
result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit details were verified from the official pages reviewed.
result.notable_signals: Official pricing publicly exposes both 'Otter API & Webhooks' and an 'Otter MCP server' feature, which is a strong signal of agent/tooling relevance even though API access is plan-gated.
result.human_review_reason: The browser observed an Otter help article describing Public API bearer-token auth, but the direct canonical article URL encountered 404 behavior during targeted recheck, so the structured auth_methods field could not be fully re-verified from a stable canonical page fetch in this pass.
